data.body:
# Canonical agent metadata: single extraction, lease-stamped, never re-derived

## Why this knot exists

This is the standing reference for "agent provider/model/version metadata
must be canonical, single-extraction, lease-authoritative." Despite many
prior fixes, the symptom keeps coming back — agent labels in the beat
table double up ("Claude Opus 4.7  Claude Opus 4.7  4.7"), versions leak
across providers ("OpenCode openrouter/moonshotai/kimi-k2.6 4.7" — the
"4.7" is the OpenCode binary version, not a model version), and the
implementation drifts because there is no single contract everyone agrees
to.

The contract is now written down:

- Foolery side: `docs/knots-agent-identity-contract.md` (rewritten 2026-04-30)
- Knots side: `~/knots/docs/leases.md` — "Agent identity propagation" (extended 2026-04-30)

This knot tracks bringing the codebase into compliance with that contract
and stays open as a reference: every future agent-metadata bug should be
diagnosed against the rules below before any code changes.

## The nine load-bearing rules

(Verbatim from the contract doc — repeated here so the knot is
self-contained and survives doc reorganisations.)

1. **Canonical extraction.** When Foolery creates a Knots lease, it
   derives `{ agent_type, provider, agent_name, lease_model, version }`
   from the registered agent config (and only from there) using one
   provider-specific parser per provider.
2. **Single moment.** Extraction happens exactly once per claim, at the
   moment Foolery calls `kno lease create`.
3. **Lease is authoritative.** From that point on, Foolery only learns
   the agent identity by reading the knot or the lease. No re-parsing
   of `command` / `model` / `version` from any other source.
4. **Knots stamps every artifact.** Knots reads the bound lease's
   `agent_info` and stamps `agent_name` / `agent_model` / `agent_version`
   onto every step-history entry, note, handoff capsule, and gate
   decision.
5. **Runtime/session metadata is autostamp-derived.** Anywhere Foolery
   displays "who is working on this beat right now," the data MUST come
   from the knot's most recent autostamped artifact. Not from environment
   variables. Not from CLI argv. Not from a parallel terminal-store
   field someone wrote at spawn time.
6. **One function per app, per task.** Extraction lives in one function.
   Resolution lives in one function. Construction of the lease payload
   lives in one function. Display formatting lives in one function. Per
   provider, per task. No duplicate branches scattered across files.
7. **Per-provider unit tests.** Each provider's extractor has a
   deterministic test covering canonical inputs.
8. **Foolery only extracts at lease setup.** No other code path may call
   the canonical extractor or its provider-specific helpers.
9. **Knots preserves lease metadata per its documented contract.** Once
   stamped, metadata travels with the artifact; Foolery treats it as
   final.

## Current state (snapshot 2026-04-30)

What's correct already:

- `src/lib/agent-identity.ts` is the per-provider parser file. It exposes
  `normalizeClaudeModel`, `normalizeCodexModel`, `normalizeGeminiModel`,
  `normalizeCopilotModel`, `normalizeAgentIdentity`,
  `toCanonicalLeaseIdentity`, `toExecutionAgentInfo`. These are the
  canonical functions.
- `src/lib/knots-lease-runtime.ts:ensureKnotsLease` is the only call
  site that builds a Knots lease and calls `createLease`.
- `src/lib/knots-operations.ts:createLease` is the only Foolery function
  that passes `--agent-name`, `--model`, `--model-version`, `--provider`,
  `--agent-type` to `kno`.
- `src/lib/__tests__/knots-canonical-metadata.test.ts` and
  `knots-coverage-crud.test.ts` / `knots-integration-operations.test.ts`
  already assert that `claimKnot`, `pollKnot`, `nextKnot`,
  `updateKnot` (note + handoff paths) do NOT include agent-identity flags.
- Knots-side `resolve_lease_agent_info` in
  `~/knots/src/write_dispatch/helpers.rs` correctly reads the bound
  lease's `agent_info` and stamps it onto every artifact type.

What's wrong (the violations to fix in this knot):

- **OpenCode has no canonical extractor.** `normalizeAgentIdentity`
  (agent-identity.ts:243-250) treats OpenCode as a passthrough for
  `model`, `flavor`, `version`. Result: the OpenCode binary version
  ("4.7") leaks into the model-version slot for an agent running
  `openrouter/moonshotai/kimi-k2.6`. There is `parseOpenCodeModelPath`
  for display (agent-identity.ts:381), but it is not used during
  extraction.
- **Re-normalisation at display time.**
  `src/app/beats/to-active-agent-info.ts:8-44` calls
  `normalizeAgentIdentity` on raw fields received at the display layer.
  This is a duplicate of the canonical extraction. It should read
  already-canonical fields off a stamped artifact.
- **Terminal store holds raw agent fields as runtime state.**
  `src/stores/terminal-store.ts:11-14` defines `agentName`, `agentModel`,
  `agentVersion`, `agentCommand` as separate persisted fields. These can
  drift from the lease. They should be replaced with `leaseId` plus a
  hook that resolves the lease's `agent_info` on demand.
- **`use-agent-info-map.ts` re-normalises.**
  `src/app/beats/use-agent-info-map.ts:33,60` reads stamped capsule
  fields (`last.agentname`, `last.model`, `last.version`) and runs them
  through `toActiveAgentInfo`, which re-runs `normalizeAgentIdentity`.
  The capsule fields are already canonical because Knots stamped them
  from the lease — they need no further normalisation.
- **Beat metadata reader uses multi-key fallbacks.**
  `src/components/beat-table-metadata.tsx:168-201` resolves agent fields
  by trying `agentname` then `agentName` then `agent_name`. This is a
  symptom of inconsistent stamping at the source. The fix is at the
  stamp site, not the read site.
- **`agent-history-utils.tsx` has its own `buildAgentLabel`.**
  (agent-history-utils.tsx:391) Yet another formatter. Should reduce to
  `formatAgentDisplayLabel` from agent-identity.ts.
- **No flow diagram in either repo until 2026-04-30.** The fix landed
  in this knot's docs updates; keep the contract doc and `leases.md` in
  sync forever.

## What "fixed" looks like

A future agent looking at this knot can verify all-clear by running the
self-audit greps in `docs/knots-agent-identity-contract.md` and seeing
zero unsanctioned hits. Each acceptance criterion below maps to a
specific failure mode of the current code.

## Cross-references

- **Contract (Foolery):** `docs/knots-agent-identity-contract.md`
- **Contract (Knots):** `~/knots/docs/leases.md` § "Agent identity propagation"
- **Per-provider parsers:** `src/lib/agent-identity.ts`
- **Lease creation:** `src/lib/knots-lease-runtime.ts`,
  `src/lib/knots-operations.ts:createLease`
- **Existing tests:** `src/lib/__tests__/knots-canonical-metadata.test.ts`
data.title: Canonical agent metadata: single extraction, lease-stamped, never re-derived
